wiki.summary: “Highway 95 pt.2” is the sequel song to “highway 95” from the deluxe edition of his debut album, The Melodic Blue, and eighth track on his sophomore album, Ca$ino, sampling Billy Stewart’s “I Do Love You”. U.S. Route 95 is a major north-south highway in the western United States, with terminals at the Mexican border (San Luis, AZ) and Canadian border (Eastport, ID). It is the longest highway in Nevada. The segment that passes through Las Vegas is also signed as I-11. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Highway 95 pt.2” is the sequel song to “highway 95” from the deluxe edition of his debut album, The Melodic Blue, and eighth track on his sophomore album, Ca$ino, sampling Billy Stewart’s “I Do Love You”. U.S. Route 95 is a major north-south highway in the western United States, with terminals at the Mexican border (San Luis, AZ) and Canadian border (Eastport, ID). It is the longest highway in Nevada. The segment that passes through Las Vegas is also signed as I-11. This track details how Keem was a runaway child from a young age, likely due to his abusive Uncle Andre and the general abusive environment. He further expresses his fears of not being able to be a good uncle himself due to this abuse. Read more on Last.fm.
